chunks.text: At two in the morning, the corridor of the emergency hospital was silent.
Suddenly the sound of a siren drew near.
The victim of a traffic accident was being brought in.
The patient was a woman in her thirties.
She was conscious, but blood kept flowing from a deep wound.
The doctor on duty decided on an immediate operation.
The nurses, with practiced motion, prepared for surgery.
Medical devices, blood for transfusion, sterilized instruments.
Not a moment of delay could be allowed.
The surgery lasted three hours.
The doctor had to concentrate and, with the greatest care, stitch each blood vessel.
Not a single symptom could be overlooked.
Eventually the surgery ended, and the doctor swept the sweat from his brow.
The patient's condition became stable.
The family, in the waiting room, shed tears and thanked him.
Recovery will take time.
But she had been saved.
Doctors, nurses, technicians.
Through the teamwork of all the hospital personnel, a single life was saved.
At medical sites, such stories are repeated every day.
Under tremendous pressure, they continue their work.
Dedication is the greatest force that supports medicine.
